steps.title: Identify the claim to support
steps.content: The student claims that Argentina and the United States produce more cheaply than India and Russia. Per the passage, when tariffs are removed, cheaper producers expand output while more expensive ones contract.

steps.title: Match the signal to the data
steps.content: Choice A directly captures the predicted contrast: production decreasing in India and Russia but increasing in Argentina and the United States — exactly the asymmetry that signals Argentina and the United States are the cheaper producers. The other choices either focus on prices (B), describe price/production effects without the cross-country contrast (C), or compare absolute output rather than the change in output (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
